fund.name: HDFC Hybrid Equity Fund - Direct Plan - Growth Option

allocations.rationale: Top-performing hybrid fund with 5/5 Morningstar rating. Allocates ~70% to equity (mostly large-cap) and ~30% to debt. Provides better returns than pure debt while preserving capital better than pure equity. Good for your 16-year horizon with conservative risk appetite.

fund.name: Nippon India Large & Midcap Fund - Direct Plan - Growth Option
fund.fund_house: Nippon Life India Asset Management Limited

allocations.rationale: Strong large-mid cap fund with 4/5 rating. Large-cap stocks provide stability, mid-caps offer growth potential. Lasts 16 years, so can ride out market ups and downs.

fund.name: HDFC Prudence Fund - Direct Plan - Growth Option

allocations.rationale: Top conservative hybrid fund with 5/5 rating. ~40% equity (mostly large-cap) + ~60% debt. Very stable - great for preserving capital while still getting modest growth. Your primary 'safe haven' fund.

fund.name: ICICI Prudential Equity & Debt - Direct Plan - Growth Option
fund.fund_house: ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: Lower-risk hybrid fund with 3/5 rating. ~60% equity + ~40% debt. Less volatile than HDFC Hybrid Equity. Good for diversification.

plan.setup_phase:
First month: Use ₹13.5 lakh lumpsum from existing corpus to buy 45% in HDFC Hybrid Equity Fund (₹13.50 L), ₹7.5 lakh in Nippon India Large & Midcap Fund (₹7.50 L), ₹6 lakh in HDFC Prudence Fund (₹6 L), and ₹3 lakh in ICICI Prudential Equity & Debt Fund (₹3 L).

Next month onwards: Start SIP of ₹30,000/month across all four funds (₹13,500 in HDFC Hybrid Equity, ₹7,500 in Nippon Large & Midcap, ₹6,000 in HDFC Prudence, ₹3,000 in ICICI Equity & Debt).
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Check if SIP is running smoothly
After 1 year: Review all fund performance and compare to category benchmarks
Every 2-3 years: Reassess if risk appetite still matches fund allocation
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Rebalance once a year. If any fund's allocation drifts more than 5% from its target, adjust by selling the over-weighted fund and buying the under-weighted one. For example, if HDFC Hybrid Equity grows to 50% of portfolio, sell ₹1.00 L from it and buy ₹1.00 L in HDFC Prudence to bring it back to 45% equity allocation.
plan.rationale: Your main goal is to protect capital while still growing your ₹30 lakh corpus. The plan uses 45% in top hybrid equity funds (good for long-term growth) and 25% in large-mid cap equity (good for riding out short-term dips). The remaining 30% is in conservative hybrid funds for stability. This mix provides 16-year growth potential while being less risky than pure equity. Regular income is generated through the fund's dividend option (not shown here) and SIP growth.
risks: Market value can drop temporarily during economic downturns—this is normal and expected to recover
If you need to withdraw money before the 16-year horizon, you might lose money in the equity portion
Inflation could erode real returns over time if growth stays below 7%
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth

plan.perspective: This plan balances protection and growth for your 16-year horizon with conservative risk appetite. The 45% equity allocation is slightly higher than average for your age group (which is 44), but paired with conservative hybrids to reduce volatility. With ₹30,000/month SIP and ₹30 lakh lumpsum, you're on track to have around ₹1.5–2.00 crore in 16 years depending on market performance. Income generation comes mainly from the hybrid funds' dividend payouts and the compounding of your SIPs.

aps.reasoning: All equity allocations are actively managed funds (Nippon Large & Midcap Fund) with no passive/index funds mentioned. Annual rebalancing suggests moderate turnover. Low cost emphasis with minimal mention of expense ratios. Heavy reliance on fund-specific research (Morningstar ratings, fund manager trackrecords). Long-term 16-year horizon with infrequent reviews. Portfolio shows genuinely active characteristics with high alpha (11.92-12.35), tracking error (3.88-4.16), and information ratio (2.89-3.19) across equity components.

pqs.reasoning: The plan shows moderate-to-good alignment with Marge's conservative profile and capital preservation goals (goal_alignment: 0.7). Diversification is strong across fund houses and hybrid categories (diversification: 0.8). However, the plan's risk profile is problematic - allocating 70% to equity/hybrid funds (including 25% pure equity) for a conservative investor who prioritizes capital safety is inappropriate (risk_return_appropriateness: 0.5). The plan is internally consistent with clear rationales and rebalancing guidelines (internal_consistency: 0.9). Tax efficiency is good for a 20% slab investor with hybrid funds receiving favorable equity taxation, though the plan doesn't explicitly mention tax considerations (tax_efficiency: 0.8).
